name: H-IIA
first: Japan's longest-serving operational launcher — flew Hayabusa, Akatsuki, and Hope Probe
description: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries' two-stage workhorse, in service de 2001 through retirement in 2024. Cryogenic core stage with 2-4 solid boosters. Notable interplanetary missões: Hayabusa (2003) and Hayabusa2 (2014) para asteroids; Akatsuki (2010) para Venus; the UAE's Hope Probe (2020) para Marte; SLIM (2023) para the Lua. Replaced by the H3.
